121524.explanation: A referendum often asks for a “yes” or “no” answer, while addressing a complex problem often requires more. Municipal council members can prepare thoroughly for complex issues and must take residents' wishes into account.

121528.explanation: CDA wants to support sustainability where possible and financially responsible.

121529.explanation: CDA does not see why this should be opposed given Opmeer's location, close to the A.C. and A7. De Veken could be a location. Yes, distribution centers require a lot of space, but for the public interest we must also look where locations are available.

121530.explanation: CDA believes the asylum reception should close after the contract ends, so in 2030. Opmeer has done its part, took a risk, where other municipalities remained inactive.

121531.explanation: CDA believes Opmeer has already taken responsibility with the reception and thinks it is now up to others to offer solutions.

121537.explanation: There are costs involved here, both for transport and incineration. The municipality cannot bear these costs, as it demands a lot from finances. This would then be offset by other cutbacks.

121539.explanation: CDA believes everyone should take responsibility for others. Entrepreneurs, like the government, must also take into account people with disabilities.

121540.explanation: Under conditions, this should be possible, but we must not return to the time when you smoked a cigar with the mayor and could build a house. Otherwise, you get legal inequality and strange housing projects in the most unlikely places.

121541.explanation: The CDA believes that no extra money should go here. The regional broadcasters are already well financed.

121543.explanation: According to the CDA, BOAs (Public Enforcement Officers) should be deployed selectively. On the other hand, there are not many problems requiring BOAs' attention. Opmeer is a relatively safe municipality. Additional tasks beyond the general ones are not needed.

121544.explanation: The CDA believes that these kinds of matters should be addressed regionally through the GGD (Public Health Service).

121550.explanation: The CDA believes that the current lower threshold is well balanced and tailored to people who really need help.

121552.explanation: The CDA believes that charging facilities should be offered, but we must remain mindful of the problems this can cause for others. We think the pace can be somewhat slowed down.

121553.explanation: 30 km/h speed limits are not necessary or meaningful everywhere. You should be able to drive through to some extent. Moreover, the number of incidents is low in Opmeer.

121554.explanation: In recent years, we have faced a tremendous amount of financial problems due to rising costs of youth care. The CDA believes there should at least be stricter management and control over the costs incurred.

121557.explanation: The tourist tax can remain as it is for now. The dog tax has been abolished and replaced by increased tourist and commuter taxes. A tourist has to pay €0.55 more per night, so €1.80 per night. This is still cheaper than municipalities in the surrounding area.

121560.explanation: Refugees with status should be assigned social rental housing. This concerns only a handful of people in Opmeer in a housing corporation with around 900 homes. This generally finds its way and does not pose a real problem.

121566.explanation: Yes, actually that is a very good idea. But if you didn’t learn it from your parents, it is incredibly difficult to teach the language to those young ones. So how do you do that then? It is better if the young ones also speak West Frisian at home.

121568.explanation: The CDA believes that if spatially possible, this should be allowed.

121569.explanation: There are sufficient parking spots in the center of Opmeer. More important is the question of how we will organize parking within the new center plan that was recently adopted.

121570.explanation: The CDA sees opportunities here to improve the shopping experience in the center. A condition is that careful attention is paid, during the further elaboration of the center plans, to how parking will be rearranged.

121571.explanation: The current designation of the recreational park is 'Recreation'. Housing as it is currently done is not allowed and must be enforced. If the recreational park itself wants it differently, it must come up with plans.

121572.explanation: A municipality cannot influence who buys what, but it can within exploitation plans and the housing vision determine what is built and at what price. This must be well aligned with demand in the area and a good mix of supply.

121574.explanation: The camera surveillance as it currently takes place is related to an addition from the CDA to the plan for the arrival of refugee shelter. We wanted to prevent not being able to monitor the situation. As it is now, it is fine. Other neighborhoods do not need camera surveillance.

121524.explanation: We will first implement the new Participation Act and then evaluate it.

121528.explanation: Energy subsidies come from the national government.

121529.explanation: Opmeer is not suitable for this.

121530.explanation: The agreement is five years. Maintain the number of 125 persons.

121531.explanation: Meeting the need. No further opinion.

121537.explanation: Is set in policy from January 1; 10 euros.

121540.explanation: Through Collective Private Commissioning. Can also apply to a private landowner?

121543.explanation: Two is enough. Deployment could be more visible.

121544.explanation: The central municipality Alkmaar is responsible for this. There is already one, after all? Together with the region, no problem.

121550.explanation: An income limit is a broad concept.

121554.explanation: Always pay attention to price and quality. We do support better supervision!

121557.explanation: In balance with comparable municipalities. The playing field for entrepreneurs must be the same.

121566.explanation: Could be nice, but then as Environmental Sciences.

121568.explanation: Can be proportionally added to the appearance of a town/village.

121570.explanation: Disabled/handicapped people must also be able to access.

121524.explanation: VVD Opmeer considers participation with stakeholders very important. On December 11, 2025, the Participation Regulation was established in the municipality. This regulation aims to safeguard the quality of local democratic processes.

121528.explanation: VVD Opmeer wants a municipal energy company and provides up to €2,000 subsidy to help homeowners insulate. We focus on nuclear energy, further developing options from recent research, and sustainable energy storage with a battery park next to De Veken 4. No solar fields and wind turbines, solar panels on roofs!

121529.explanation: VVD Opmeer wants to preserve and strengthen the manufacturing industry at the De Veken industrial area. Urgently develop De Veken 4.

121530.explanation: VVD Opmeer adheres to the law. Once the agreement ends, the asylum shelter must close immediately. We also no longer want to prioritize status holders for social rental housing – not even with urgency. Our own residents first!

121531.explanation: VVD Opmeer adheres to the law. Once the agreement ends, the asylum shelter must close immediately. We also no longer want to prioritize status holders for social rental housing – not even with urgency. Our own residents first!

121537.explanation: VVD Opmeer does not want a recycling fee. No paying for residual waste by kilo weight to HVC. Charging €10 raises awareness that collecting bulky residual waste is an expensive activity and does not contribute to separating residual waste. The actual costs are much higher.

121539.explanation: VVD Opmeer invests in the “Small Villages Approach” for Aartswoud and De Weere. Entrepreneurs themselves are aware of inclusion and sustainable entrepreneurship; this is part of good business management. The municipality facilitates and supports entrepreneurs by connecting parties.

121540.explanation: VVD Opmeer wants to allow building of care homes without permits and provide more space for adding floors and splitting homes. Promote housing flow by helping seniors with relocation. Targeted housing allocation and more construction with priority for our own residents. BUILD! Also upwards.

121541.explanation: VVD Opmeer stands for transparent communication in language understandable to everyone. The local regional broadcaster plays an important role in our free democracy with the fulfillment of three core tasks: news, education, and journalism. With VVD Westfriesland “Building together for a strong region.”

121543.explanation: VVD Opmeer wants more BOAs (at least 2) to tackle nuisance offenders and prevent undermining. Everyone should feel safe on and around the streets. CCTV camera surveillance is a tool needed to make enforcement of street intimidation offenses even more effective.

121544.explanation: VVD Opmeer wants to continue cooperation with all municipalities in North Holland North with Veilig Thuis NHN. Veilig Thuis offers help to restore safety with local teams such as the WMO counters. Veilig Thuis arranges help and support for families and works closely with the municipality’s Neighborhood Team.

121550.explanation: VVD Opmeer wants to involve schools in early detection of problems and reducing health disparities. The municipal social assistance standard of 120% is already higher. Our municipality’s Neighborhood Team reviews the full financial situation to determine eligibility for benefits.

121552.explanation: VVD Opmeer wants to accelerate the installation of municipal charging points for both cars and bicycles, so that within 300 meters of every home there is a public charging station.

121553.explanation: VVD Opmeer wants to better protect cyclists and pedestrians by creating bike lanes and walking paths. Increase safety with smart, sustainable public lighting in rural areas like Oosterboekelweg towards De Weere. Lower the speed limit to 30 km/h on the Spanbroekerweg within the built-up area.

121554.explanation: VVD Opmeer focuses on prevention. Reducing health disparities, investing in healthy food at schools, and offering a free sports and music pass for youth and young people so that sports and exercise remain accessible and affordable 365 days a year. Combating loneliness and child poverty in the neighborhood.

121557.explanation: VVD Opmeer wants to expand recreational opportunities and facilities for campgrounds and at the Spanbroek guest harbor. Taxes and fees, including property tax, as low and fair as possible.

121560.explanation: VVD Opmeer does not want to prioritize status holders for social rental housing – not even with urgency. An urgency scheme where divorced residents have priority for their own housing. Targeted housing allocation and more construction with priority for our own residents. Own residents first.

121566.explanation: VVD Opmeer puts Opmeer on the map by emphasizing our culture, landscape, walking and cycling routes. Our culture should be part of all primary schools. Giving more space to libraries is also part of this.

121568.explanation: VVD Opmeer wants to BUILD! Also upwards. Provide space for adding floors and splitting homes. Encourage building between existing buildings and locations for infill development. Develop homes at Oeverval Hoogwoud (CPO) and Spanbroek South. This way, farmers get space to produce, innovate and compete.

121569.explanation: VVD Opmeer wants a shopping area with a pleasant and livable environment where cyclists and pedestrians are better protected. Expand public transport while maintaining connections to Obdam, Hoorn and the community bus. Nearly 100 underground parking spaces will be added in the Koenis development.

121570.explanation: VVD Opmeer wants a shopping area with a pleasant and livable environment where cyclists and pedestrians are better protected. By conducting research in the center, we can together with entrepreneurs and residents determine which traffic circulation plan offers the best solution for the future.

121571.explanation: VVD Opmeer wants to issue a personal tolerance permit as soon as the Homeowners' Association has concluded a preliminary agreement. Current owners and renters of a recreational home can then submit an application.

121572.explanation: VVD Opmeer wants to help youth and first-time buyers purchase their first home and offer a starter loan only to residents with local ties. Targeted housing allocation and more construction with priority for our own residents. We promote housing flow by helping seniors with relocation.

121574.explanation: VVD Opmeer wants safety on and around the streets; we want more BOAs (at least 2) to tackle nuisance offenders. More camera surveillance is needed to make enforcement of street intimidation offenses even more effective. CCTV camera surveillance helps people feel safer on the streets.

121524.explanation: Participation is conducted depending on the subject. A referendum is among the possibilities if appropriate.

121528.explanation: This is the ideal picture: residents jointly produce their own green and affordable electricity with a wind turbine or solar panels. They use the electricity onsite, so the grid is not burdened. Energy cooperatives are an asset to our municipality and should be encouraged.

121529.explanation: The business park near Opmeer (De Veken) is not designated for large-scale distribution centers. This will also not happen during the further development of De Veken 4.

121530.explanation: The asylum shelter in Opmeer is running well and remains in place according to the contract with the COA (Central Reception of Asylum Seekers).

121531.explanation: There is an AZC (asylum seeker center) in Opmeer. It operates properly.

121537.explanation: Bulky waste on the street is annoying, unsafe and unhealthy. The easiest way to prevent this is by collecting bulky waste free of charge. This reduces hassle and results in cleaner streets for us.

121539.explanation: Accessibility is vital for people with disabilities. However, many important places are not yet properly accessible, for example for wheelchair users. Entrepreneurs often cannot afford such adaptations on their own. Therefore, we believe the municipality should provide support.

121540.explanation: There is a huge housing shortage and an extra home on the property can be a good solution. As an additional income source or as a suitable place for a parent who can no longer live independently. We want to make this possible for farmers who want it.

121541.explanation: The current contribution to the local broadcaster is sufficient. The local broadcaster is one of the important media sources for Opmeer News.

121543.explanation: In Opmeer, BOAs already have this assignment. The scope of BOA deployment must be proportional to the problems. If these increase, more deployment is necessary. This should be reviewed annually.

121544.explanation: With the West Frisian municipalities, investment is being made in a women's shelter. Opmeer contributes financially. The shelter does not necessarily have to be located in Opmeer.

121550.explanation: The income threshold in Opmeer is fine. Only if it appears that more and more people are facing problems should raising the income threshold be considered.

121552.explanation: The number of parking spaces with charging points must grow in line with demand and need. We will continue to assess whether additional charging points are needed.

121553.explanation: That is already in Opmeer and must remain. With a lower speed limit, traffic in our municipality is much safer. For cycling children and elderly with reduced mobility this makes a huge difference. Furthermore, the streets are quieter and the air cleaner.

121554.explanation: Demand is rising and so are the costs. These must be reduced. There must be strict tendering and evaluation of treatment effects. We must ensure certainty and provide help that addresses our youth’s concerns to avoid the opposite effect and even higher costs.

121557.explanation: Tourist interest in Opmeer is very low. It has recently increased somewhat. This must not come at the expense of the tourism entrepreneurs themselves. They act as intermediaries and their rates should be lowered because fewer guests will then attend.

121560.explanation: Currently it is a national obligation to prioritize housing status holders. In Opmeer, this concerns about 5-7 homes per year. 50% of homes are already given to local residents. For the remaining 50%, 5-7 homes go to status holders.

121566.explanation: Attention must be paid to West Frisian culture and history. Attention can also be given to the West Frisian dialect, but it does not have to be taught as a language course.

121568.explanation: There will be one tall residential tower in the center of Opmeer as a reminder of the tower of the Koenis animal feed factory.

121569.explanation: There are currently sufficient parking spaces, with an occupancy of about 60%. For new facilities, an appropriate number of additional parking spaces must be created.

121570.explanation: This is the most ideal situation for designing an attractive, green, and safe center. If there are bottlenecks for entrepreneurs in the center, a suitable solution must be sought with them.

121571.explanation: Permanent residence is only possible if the municipality and the Homeowners' Association sign the intent to transform the recreation park into a residential area. If this does not happen, permanent living remains illegal.

121572.explanation: That depends on the location and nature of the neighborhood. Village centers will have residential circles with more homes for the elderly. At the dog walking field, more homes for starters. Hoogwoud-South will become a mixed neighborhood with more families/middle incomes. Thus, the distribution is customized per housing project.

121574.explanation: Currently, there is no reason for this in Opmeer. It is important to feel safe on the street. We do not see more cameras as the right solution. Privacy is a great good that should not be compromised lightly. Therefore, we prefer more enforcement officers on the street rather than expanding cameras.

121531.explanation: Not applicable; we already have it.

121537.explanation: Once a year for free on a fixed date.

121560.explanation: In order of the waiting list.

121524.explanation: We are proponents of direct democracy and think tanks: involve residents early through consultations and panels for better, widely supported decisions.

121528.explanation: Local cooperatives suit realistic sustainability: a support center for rooftop solar and small-scale initiatives, with local ownership.

121529.explanation: Space for economy with sense: allow if it fits local employment and infrastructure, with clear conditions and support.

121530.explanation: Nearby and humane: assess shelter feasibility with attention to integration, respect for local culture and local capacity, without automatic closure.

121531.explanation: Independent Opmeer: no new asylum seekers’ center without support, capacity, and direct democracy; priority to local housing shortage.

121537.explanation: Burden reduction: keeping waste disposal free stimulates proper waste management; benchmark for cost coverage.

121539.explanation: Primarily the responsibility of the entrepreneur. Connection to public infrastructure by the municipality.

121540.explanation: No construction on agricultural land: priority to preserving open space and vital centers. However, allow estate transition on farmyards for courtyards, focused on seniors, starters, and landscape integration.

121541.explanation: Sound finances: no extra subsidies without demonstrable results; priority to core facilities.

121543.explanation: Result-oriented: more enforcement officers only if necessary, focusing on prevention and efficiency through digitization.

121544.explanation: Protection of victims is crucial, but this is better and safer organized regionally.

121550.explanation: Working poor too often fall outside schemes. We raise the limit and simplify the system so aid becomes faster and more efficient.

121552.explanation: Realistic sustainability: expand charging infrastructure with smart planning.

121553.explanation: 30 km/h where it can demonstrably improve safety. Especially around schools and shopping centers.

121554.explanation: Select youth care based on waiting times, quality, and results, not primarily on price. Pay based on outcomes through result contracts, focusing on prevention and local expertise for better care.

121557.explanation: Use proceeds earmarked for recreational purposes. Stimulate the economy.

121560.explanation: Local priority: priority to Opmeerders; integration should be supported.

121566.explanation: Westfries is cultural heritage. Schools may voluntarily decide whether to teach it themselves.

121568.explanation: No high-rise flats as a goal in themselves. Height must fit the location and scale: prefer medium-height buildings near facilities and good integration over towers that spoil the village character.

121569.explanation: Accessible center: parking as a basic condition for vitality.

121570.explanation: Balance: car-reduced areas are okay, but accessibility must be maintained for businesses and residents.

121571.explanation: Permanent residence is only allowed if safety, facilities, and legal conditions are in order. The impact and obstacles for the De Veken industrial area and its entrepreneurs must be clear. The resulting costs for the municipality of legalizing must also be transparent.

121572.explanation: Starters and middle incomes are the primary target groups, as are seniors.

121574.explanation: Undesirable from a privacy point of view. But if safety considerations provide concrete reasons, this can be considered as a measure.

statements.title: The municipality should extend CCTV surveillance and expand it to all village centers.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

titleParts.text: verlengen én uitbreiden naar alle dorpscentra.

statements.title: An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: Residents of Opmeer should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Opmeer moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: Opmeer should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the street.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The tourist tax should be lowered.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: Opmeer should have a women's shelter for victims of domestic violence.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
There should be more parking spaces in the center of Opmeer.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
In new residential areas at least 40% of the homes should be for starters and middle-income households.

titleParts.text: In nieuwe woonwijken moet minstens 40% van de woningen naar

titleParts.glossary: People buying a house for the first time who have never had a mortgage.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
High-rise residential towers may be built in Opmeer.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to include the West Frisian dialect in their teaching.
The center of Opmeer should become car-free.
The municipality should allow permanent residence at Recreation Park West-Friesland.
